title: Winsth: Regeringen använder för starka ord
summary_md:
# Winsth: Regeringen använder för starka ord

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-04-27  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A macro-focused episode where Nordea chief economist Annika Winsth and DNB's Ulf Andersson argue that the Swedish government and Riksbank are using overly alarming language (stagflation, serious) about an economy that still has solid fundamentals. They expect continued GDP growth (~2%), below-target inflation aided by a stronger krona, and at most one rate hike late in the year, contingent on the Hormuz strait reopening before summer. Swedish exporters and industrials remain competitive and well positioned despite global uncertainty.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟠 VOLV-B — Volvo | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Industrials delivering strong earnings and good order books; Swedish exporters competitive despite stronger krona and trade disruption.

#### Reasoning

The hosts highlight verkstad (industrials) including Volvo as delivering very fine figures with healthy order situations, and praise Swedish exporters for outperforming world trade despite a stronger currency. This is positive macro/sector commentary rather than a stock-specific buy thesis, so the directional view is a mild positive watch.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Hormuz strait stays closed and oil spikes to ~125 USD causing stagflation
- if order intake weakens in coming quarters
- if krona strengthens further hurting export margins

> *"Verkstad levererar ju väldigt fina siffror. Årdläget för Volvo. ABB till exempel. Det finns ju inte en ton där om att det finns en oro finns det. Men det syns ju inte än så länge."*

---

### 🟠 ABB — ABB | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Named with Volvo as an industrial delivering fine numbers; structural demand from defence, infrastructure and energy transition supports the sector.

#### Reasoning

ABB is cited as an example of Swedish industrials posting strong results with no visible worry yet. The hosts also point to multi-year structural investment demand (defence, infrastructure, energy transition) that should support industrials, giving a mildly positive but non-specific directional view.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Hormuz closure triggers stagflation and sharply higher rates
- if structural investment demand fails to materialise
- if global industrial demand rolls over

> *"Verkstad levererar ju väldigt fina siffror. Årdläget för Volvo. ABB till exempel."*

---
